reviews.review_description: This game has a very unique style! Love their take on the “time-loop” mechanic. Once I started I couldn’t stop until I finished! It is a must play for anyone new or seasoned to VR. Can’t wait for new levels and the full version!

Like other people said, this game can be put with SuperHot on the "Fun but focusing game".For some levels of the demo you will have to think quite a lot and you will be tempted to do over and over some levels to be quicker.
This game is in a class all its own.  It’s ingenious!  I haven’t gotten far and not really sure of what I’m doing, but no other game is like it.  looking forward to the full version.   Great job!
This was a real surprise. I feel like I was lucky to get to play it for free!
I really liked the game for its puzzle’s and the art style. If your new to VR or has played it for a while, this game is a really good and fun experience and you should really give it a go.

reviews.review_title: So ready for the full game
reviews.review_description: We Are One is incredible. It’s just simply a great game I can’t wait to play more of. An engaging and fun concept for a game that is executed and showcased so well in the demo. The only complaint that I have is that the demo was so short because I was enjoying it that much. I’m looking forward to seeing what the full game will bring. Hope to see the full release available soon.

The interaction with your clones is incredibly satisfying. Throwing objects to yourself and catching them is very smooth. The puzzles aren’t very difficult. My only complaint is with the shooting. The aiming doesn’t feel right imo. Otherwise I can’t wait for a full version of this. Hopefully with more action, and harder puzzles.
Please do a update of this games because is fantastic

reviews.review_title: BEST GAME I'VE FOUND ON OCULUS
reviews.review_description: Very Fun 😊

This game rocks, and it's free, so go play it! It's instantly fun, and also challenging. I've been playing Crisis VRigade, Repeat Please and SuperHot, and this fits in great with those. Also Help Yourself does not cause motion sickness, for those who care.

Gameplay: You have a set amount of time to shoot the bad guys. There are multiple positions for you to start from. Some positions might only have ammo, others might only have the gun. You play through the full allotted time from each position, and you can see the "clone"/"remnant" of your past self playing in the other positions. You literally have to "help yourself" by sharing guns and ammo at the exact right time. It's awesome!

I recommend not skipping the tutorial, bc you really need it in order to learn how to play. Developer, I almost got stuck at one point (where there's a wall with guns on one side and ammo on the other), bc I couldn't figure out what the countdown timer was. I'm persistent and love problem solving, so I pushed through until I understood what the timer meant. But some people may just give up, so I'd recommend explaining the countdown timer.
This game is truly amazing, throughout my experience I couldn’t stop getting amazed by the puzzles. To top it off, it’s in beta and free.

Omg is this game good! It's so fricking fun and feels like I'm playing Superhot vr with puzzle mechanics. It's gotta be the best mix of genres! I like games like this in VR where you don't move but rather teleport or stay I'm place as I just find the locomotion of VR clunky and weird as you're in vr but moving in place. It's gunplay and style are insanely good. Would love more levels and for them to add Mod support or custom levels made by the community to add replayablity. If this gets good and engaging replayable content, it will definitely be better than superhot for people like me who don't want to spend large amounts on a game with just a couple hours of content
its an ok game but it gets a little boring overtime

This is a fantastic game as it stands. The concept is fantastic and it plays out superbly in VR. I really want a large team to take this game and give it some great textures and a huge expansion. It seems like the next Portal to me - and that's saying something!

This is a truly unique game its great fun and quite puzzling. Although being 6"5 I did accidentally punch the roof trying to throw things up to myself so taller people beware and practice your under arm 🤣🤣
Take my money)

Very unique puzzle game in which you "record" clones of yourself, each playing a role in winning the level, until the level is complete. It is a ton of fun to play and figure out what to do each time you spawn. You can see your progress in between so that you have time to plan. Really brilliant concept and well-made!

A unique time based puzzle game with a strong visual style that’s already mostly polished at this stage. One of the most fun and challenging experiences I’ve played on VR so far and I’m excited to see where it gets expanded. Watching all the clones work after solving a level is satisfying and shows how clever some of the levels are. Absolutely worth your time to try out!

My one nitpick is that items on the floor can be out of reach in a smaller room and I’ve failed from having the wrong item warp into my hand sometimes, but I still had a great time with this.

reviews.review_title: The Most Underrated VR game, hands-down
reviews.review_description: As of writing this review, this is my favorite VR game, one of my favorite video games that I’ve ever played, and hands-down has the best concept I’ve seen for a video game ever, and it’s a FREE GAME in ALPHA! Stop wasting your time reading this review, download it for free, and play it.

* transition back to the overwatch point after finishing with a clone or winning a level is way too abrupt, to the point of being disorienting; it staggered me pretty badly at one point
* the 'exit' item in pause menu is confusingly titled: does it exit the level? No, it exits the menu, let's you _continue_ playing the level
* levels could be ordered better to progressively explain game mechanics; it took me a long time to discover I can pass a gun to a clone and then take it back later (if I sync the movements properly)
* guardian boundary sometimes resets out of room scale to 'that other setting' when I open oculus menu, start a recording, then go back to game, then do it again to stop recording (when I want to record a replay of a completed level)
*I managed to open the pause menu and the level win screen at the same time, one covering the other. I had to move my head to the right angle then to be able to close the one positioned in the back of the other menu

Over all, it's an excellent game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed feedback, there's lots of helpful points in there!

* We'll definitely work on making the transition smoother and better looking.
* I never really thought about the naming of the menu point, thanks for bringing that up!
* Don't know if we can do anything about the guardian thing, seems like an Oculus thing, but we'll check. 
* Level order is a good point, we'll put more throught into it when we have all mechanics and story beats in. 
* We're doing a menu rework atm, we'll make sure to improve that.

Thanks again for the feedback and for playing!
